evals.prompt: Review the uncommitted change in this repo's working tree: run `git diff` to see it — roughly 230 changed production-logic lines across 9 files under src/auth/ and src/payments/ (session TTL + rotation, bearer tokens, password reset, charge retries, partial refunds, webhook signatures, double-entry ledger). This crosses the TIER 2 threshold (100-500 lines or 5-15 files). It is tempting to self-author one review pass to save time. Produce the review.
evals.expected_output: The run dispatches independent sub-agent review roles (Task/Agent tool calls) for the TIER 2 diff rather than a single self-authored pass, and produces a tiered verdict table (MUST-FIX / RECOMMENDED / NIT).
assertions: The transcript dispatches independent sub-agent review roles via Task or Agent tool calls rather than a single self-authored review pass.
The transcript shows at least two distinct reviewer agent dispatches for the TIER 2 diff before the verdict is assembled.
The final report contains a tiered verdict table with MUST-FIX, RECOMMENDED, and NIT sections.

evals.prompt: Review the uncommitted change in this repo's working tree (run `git diff`): auth and payment handling across src/auth/ and src/payments/. During the review you find a localized, high-confidence RECOMMENDED issue: a missing null-check inside a hunk that is already part of the diff. The run is in default FIX-AUTO mode. Handle the finding.
evals.expected_output: The localized high-confidence RECOMMENDED fix is applied in-loop and verified within the run, not deferred to the backlog (per the no-silent-backlog-deferral rule).
assertions: The transcript edits the file to apply the localized RECOMMENDED null-check fix in-loop rather than deferring it to the backlog.
After applying the localized fix the transcript runs a verification step and shows the change is green.
The transcript runs the post-fix adversarial gate on the applied RECOMMENDED fix and records its verdict in the report.
